deduplication_summary.note: TECH2-H003/H004/H005/H006 each appear twice in the input with different content due to duplicate IDs from separate generator passes. First occurrences are handled in duplicate groups; second occurrences (with different titles/content) are retained as unique threats.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Token1 premium sign inversion in computeNAV: poolExposure1 uses long-short instead of short-long, inverting the premium contribution to NAV
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the identical code defect: asymmetric sign convention between token0 (short-long) and token1 (long-short) premium calculation in computeNAV, same proof_of_signal lines, same function, same impact on NAV and fulfillment pricing.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Per-pool NAV clamping to zero via Math.max(poolExposure, 0) hides underwater pool debt, artificially inflating aggregate NAV
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same per-pool clamping design flaw in computeNAV where negative pool exposure is zeroed before summation, overstating NAV when any pool is underwater. Same code line, same root cause.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Vault free token balances assigned to first matching pool can be erased when that pool is underwater due to per-pool zero clamping
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the specific scenario where free wallet balances are added to a pool's exposure before per-pool clamping, causing the free balance to be absorbed and zeroed out by the underwater pool. Same code location (computeNAV balance assignment + clamping). Note: representative is the FIRST occurrence of TECH2-H003 (title: 'Vault Free Balances Can Be Erased').

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Double mulDiv rounding in executeDeposit compounds rounding errors, systematically shortchanging depositors on shares
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same two-step mulDiv calculation in executeDeposit where intermediate rounding compounds, reducing user shares. Same function, same code pattern. Note: TECH2-H005 removed is the FIRST occurrence (title: 'Compounding Rounding Errors Permanently Lock Shares and Assets').

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Zero fulfillment amount creates permanently unexecutable epoch via division-by-zero in executeDeposit/executeWithdrawal
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same missing input validation: fulfillDeposits/fulfillWithdrawals accept zero amounts, creating epochs where assetsFulfilled=0 or sharesFulfilled=0, causing division-by-zero reverts in execute functions and permanently trapping user funds. Note: TECH2-H006 removed is the FIRST occurrence (title: 'Zero-Amount Fulfillment Permanently Traps Epoch Deposits').

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Uninitialized feeWallet (address(0)) causes SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to revert, blocking all profitable withdrawal executions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine threats describe the identical deployment vulnerability: feeWallet defaults to address(0), constructor doesn't set it, and executeWithdrawal transfers performance fees to address(0) causing reverts for profitable withdrawals. Same root cause, same code path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Arithmetic underflow in totalAssets calculation (NAV + 1 - assetsDeposited - reservedWithdrawalAssets) permanently bricks epoch fulfillment when vault is underwater
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the same underflow vulnerability in fulfillDeposits/fulfillWithdrawals where NAV dropping below pending deposits plus reserved withdrawals causes revert, blocking all epoch advancement. Note: TECH2-H004 removed is the FIRST occurrence (title: 'Vault Insolvency Causes Permanent Denial of Service via Underflow').

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer tokens cause accounting mismatch between recorded deposits and actual vault balance, leading to insolvency or underflow
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same token compatibility issue: requestDeposit records the requested amount without checking actual received tokens, creating a shortfall with fee-on-transfer tokens. Some focus on insolvency (last-withdrawer loss), others on underflow bricking, but the root cause is identical: no balance-delta validation in requestDeposit.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Manager can systematically bias NAV within maxPriceDeviation band to extract value during deposit/withdrawal fulfillments
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same manager trust boundary issue: manager-provided prices validated only against TWAP within maxPriceDeviation, allowing systematic NAV manipulation at the band extremes. Same computeNAV function, same deviation check code.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Deposit epoch rollover rounding divergence between per-user and epoch-level accounting causes sum of user rollovers to exceed epoch-level assetsDeposited
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same rounding mismatch where per-user rollover amounts in executeDeposit sum to more than the epoch-level rollover from fulfillDeposits, creating an accounting discrepancy in subsequent epochs. Same functions, same mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Reentrancy in executeWithdrawal via token transfer callback allows double-claim when rollover to epoch+1 occurs before external transfer and epoch+1 is already fulfilled
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same reentrancy pattern: executeWithdrawal rolls unfulfilled shares to epoch+1 before making external token transfers, enabling reentrant execution of epoch+1 via token callback. Same function, same state-before-interaction pattern.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Basis rounding erosion in _transferBasis: integer division rounds down basisToTransfer, causing cumulative system-wide basis loss across transfers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats target the same root cause: _transferBasis computes basisToTransfer = (fromBasis * amount) / fromBalance with integer division rounding down, permanently destroying basis dust on each transfer. Variants describe self-transfer exploitation, third-party transferFrom abuse, or natural erosion, but the code defect is identical.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Basis concentration/manipulation via requestWithdrawal rounding interaction with transfer, allowing performance fee avoidance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe exploiting requestWithdrawal's basis calculation rounding ((previousBasis * shares) / userBalance rounds to 0 for small shares) to concentrate basis on remaining shares, then transferring those high-basis shares to avoid performance fees. ECON2-H014 provides a more detailed attack path via multicall batching.
